notes: [Tree of life = T/L Tree of Knowledge of Good and Evil=TKGE] Which tree could they eat all they wanted? T/L. They couldn't eat the tree of the fruit of TKGE. What is the condition of the Garden of Eden and Adam and Eve?
touch [naga` to reach, extend to] "The fence around the law" -- Eve adds her own provision beyond God's dictum: "nor shall you touch it".
Serpent drops "LORD" from "LORD God" - LORD is Jehovah.
Satan then continues by contradicting God's word: "You will not die." God is not trustworthy. His strategy is the same today.

quotes.text: There are the two ways. What is Satan's method? Lies, deceit, and blindness. Who does he get a hold of? How? If you don't hearken to the voice of the Spirit, then Satan gets hold of you. What we have here is called REJECTION-SUBSTITUTION. This means, if you reject what God tells you to do; you will substitute something else in its place. God tells you the truth and cannot lie. At one end of the law you have truth, the other, error. If you choose not to follow God, you reject his voice, and you will substitute something else. Will the substitution work to either make you happy, or get you into the Kingdom of God? No. If you reject God and allow a substitute, it is Satan who is there to teach you the substitutions. If you don't hearken to the voice of the Spirit, you are immediately in his power. If you don't do it the Lord's way, you will do it Satan's way.
quotes.speaker: Robert Norman
quotes.attr: Robert Norman, Old Testament Class Notes

notes: Satan presents God as stingy. Parallel between this interaction and Jesus' temptation by Satan: Satan raises doubts about God' goodness, misquotes God's words.
eyes [`ayin of mental and spiritual faculties (fig.); spring, fountain]

quotes.text: Adam did not commit sin in eating the fruits, for God had decreed that he should eat and fall. But in compliance with the decree, he should die. Only [that] he should die was the saying of the Lord; therefore the Lord appointed us to fall and also redeemed us.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Smith's Commentary on the Bible
quotes.attr: Joseph Smith's Commentary on the Bible

notes: aprons [chagowr loin-covering] from "to wrap around oneself"

quotes.text: ADAM AND EVE'S TRANSGRESSION WAS NOT SEXUAL First, remember the commandment. They were commanded first to multiply and replenish the earth. There are 613 commandments in the Old Testament, according to Jewish rabbis, and this was the first. Replenish in Hebrew is "malaph" which meant "to fill up". They were to fill up the earth. How can man and woman sin sexually if God commands them to have children? Recall D&C 29 - God has never given a temporal commandment to man. Sex is considered by the Lord as spiritual, and not carnal, sensual or devilish. It must be, however, within the bounds and relationships which the Lord has established. God has set bounds on reproduction. [There are types and shadows to this, as well] The processes of sex are good and of God, and not evil. Those who advocate a life of celibacy are wrong. No rabbi ever felt that way. They felt that men weren't men until they were married. Jews were duty bound to be married. When the Lord put Adam and Eve together, and made them ONE. When they were separated they were 1/2, each. The Lord married them, in the Garden of Eden.
quotes.speaker: Robert Norman
quotes.attr: Robert Norman, Old Testament Class Notes

notes: This is the great question - where are we in relationship to God? Certainly God knew, but wanted Adam and Eve to consider where they were.
"The woman whom you gave to be with me": Could it be that Adam is blaming God and calling into question his good gift of Eve?

Eve, too, tries to shift blame, blaming the serpent as tricky. "The serpent tricked me": she is no longer naive; she knows what it means to be tricked. She has grown up a little in this. Her eyes have been opened (3:7).
"What is it that you have done?" This same question God asks Cain (4:10).
beguiled [nasha' deceive, to seize, to impose a debt, to cause to bear]
cursed ['arar have bitterness] The serpent is cursed with what he must eat. Serpent tricked them to eat. The serpent and the ground is cursed, but people aren't cursed, though their lives get harder.
seed singular -- "offspring": prefiguring Christ, who delivered the mortal blow to Satan but was wounded and died in the struggle.
heel [`aqeb footprint; to hold back (fig)]. a blow to the head is fatal, a wound in the heal is limiting but not fatal.
"you": this isn't about enmity between people and snakes, but about the Savior and Satan.
rule [mashal have dominion, preside (ie. guard - watch over)]

quotes.text: Was Eve deceived? Yes, but still we don't know what she knew before that occurred. There is language that the two trees were in the midst of the garden. That is, they had high profiles. Both were there to be seen. We are also told in the Book of Mormon, that if they had eaten right away from the Tree of Life, they would have lived forever in their sins. (See Alma 12:21-23) So they had to be cut off right away, and put out of the garden. They would now die the temporal death, and the spiritual death. Before they were put out, they were clothed. Remember that the Garden of Eden is the first temple of the earth. They have instruction from God, they are clothed, and they are told what to do, and then they had to leave. There is another veil pulled over at that time, and they enter into a world totally different from the one they left. We should empathize with them a little more. Living in a Paradise beyond our comprehension, we think about moving to that one eventually, and have hope that it will be better. They had been there, and lost light in leaving. They went the opposite direction. They knew what they lost. It was very painful. We voluntarily agreed to come here, and we knew the conditions of the plan. We agreed to come through Adam.
quotes.speaker: Robert Norman
quotes.attr: Robert Norman, Old Testament Class Notes

notes: CURSE OR BLESSING? One final comment on the CURSE on Adam and Eve. There is a double entendre concerning the curse of childbirth and working the ground. In Hebrew, in essence, he is talking to Eve and says I will "increase your distress and painful toil" in childbirth. The interesting thing about this is there is no reference to "sorrow". I don't know where that comes from. The same word for distress and painful toil is also from the word "SHAME." Rather than a curse, it appears more to be a description of telestial
"cursed is the ground": toil, pain, death enters the world. Scarcity enters, abundance leaves. They are then forced to leave the garden, representing abundance.
sweat [ze`ah from a root meaning to tremble, quiver, quake, be in terror]
bread [lechem from the root to fight, do battle, make war]
Eve [Chavvah from the root chavah implying life or living]

God gives an animal's death to cover over the effect of Adam and Eve's sin, the first sacrificial "covering" for sin, a type of Christ. Without death, God cannot redeem. So animals lost their lives as a result of the fall -- all creation falls and experiences death.
coats [kethoneth under-garment; a long shirt-like garment usually of linen]
clothed [labash to put on, be clothed with (the Greek word is enduo)]

quotes.text: The Lord clothed Adam and Eve with garments of skins (Gen. 3:21a) as coverings for their naked bodies. The Hebrew word translated coats is really the name of a garment worn next to the skin.
quotes.speaker: Ellis T. Rasmussen
quotes.source: A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Ellis T. Rasmussen, A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament

notes: Tilling the ground -- Adam's purpose in being sent from the garden.
sword [chereb (it is the same word that is used for the instrument that was used for circumcision) this implies a covenant]
We will never wander back to paradise. Socialism, communism, democratic utopianism, etc.

quotes.text: Ever since the supernatural guardians were placed at the gateway to Eden, the entrances to holy places in many ancient cultures have been guarded by some form of cherubim (already a plural, without adding s). The meaning of the word cherub (singular) is not precisely known, but the ancient statues representing cherubim were usually composite creatures with parts from the bull, lion, eagle, and so on to symbolize superior powers (BD and TG, "Cherubim").
quotes.speaker: Ellis T. Rasmussen
quotes.source: A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Ellis T. Rasmussen, A Latter-day Saint Commentary on the Old Testament

quotes.text: Instead of the tree of life, God gave Adam the Torah, which is likewise a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her, and he was permitted to take up his abode in the vicinity of Paradise in the east.
quotes.speaker: Louis Ginzberg
quotes.attr: Louis Ginzberg, Legends of the Jews, p. 81
